summary: Arista Networks and Drivetrain (Driveats/Drivets) are high-conviction positions in the AI networking infrastructure supercycle thesis — reinventing networking for disaggregated AI inference workloads.
summary_extended: Baker argues that the AI infrastructure buildout is driving a multi-year supercycle in datacenter networking, specifically because disaggregated AI inference — where pre-fill and decode operations run on different chips — requires fundamentally new networking architectures to orchestrate heterogeneous silicon. He identifies Arista Networks (ANET) as one of two high-conviction public positions he has taken in this theme, crediting the company with reinventing networking for these new AI workloads. The second company he references ('Drivets' or similar) appears to be a private networking company and thus has no US-listed ticker to audit. Baker's investment implication is that traditional networking approaches are insufficient for disaggregated inference at scale, making companies that can architect AI-native networking solutions long-term structural winners. Gerstner corroborates Baker's early and high-conviction framing of this thesis, suggesting it is a foundational multi-year view rather than a near-term trade.
detail: Baker names Arya (likely Arista Networks, ANET) and Drivets (likely a private networking company) as companies he has been publicly involved in. As data centers become more specialized with disaggregated inference (pre-fill and decode on different chips), networking must be reinvented to orchestrate heterogeneous silicon. Baker frames this as a multi-year supercycle in infra networking.
confidence_evidence: Baker says 'two names that we've been involved in publicly' and Gerstner credits Baker as 'one of the earliest' to frame the infra networking supercycle thesis.
quote_fragment: reinvent networking and Arya and driveets are coming at it in a very different way

ticker_audit.reasoning: Arista Networks is explicitly named by Baker as one of his two public high-conviction positions in the AI networking infrastructure supercycle thesis, making it a direct subject of this thesis.
